ab: Duuq heza su-u el-u-an haip?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: Miku-aq tu?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: Asa saak mintivi su-u.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: Uka saak ilumaq i heza naak a na asa-un palkada-an. Na muqna in is.
ch: W:我今天有事不在家。改天吧。
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: Duuq heza su-u el-u-an is kutun in? Na kilim saak su-u kis tonquvali in.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去找你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: Uu, na ilumaq saak maltatala su-u.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: Duuq maqtu saak siza qalinga? Na asa saak matsulut mapatas.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: Maqtu!
ch: W:好啊!沒有問題！
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: Duuq maqtu siza ki bisukbisuk tu qaningu?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: Azuq ni-i, mopa ni-i ang saak mapinqala, ni-i ang amin patqemansutan a sankunan.
ch: W:我不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要啦。
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: Uu, na lusqa saak siza qaningu.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: Azuq amin ni-i tu siza-an i qaningu.
ch: W:拍照也不要。
en: No photographing either.

ab: Unin su-u ki maqtu ang mun-iti pababazbaz.
ch: M:謝謝你讓我訪問。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: Ni-i tu miku-aq, ma-aq su-u asa-un istupa?
ch: W:沒問題，你想問什麼?
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: Taqu-av ang tu malkamama-aq asu tu mendudu-az ang?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: Makakeval saak tu qabas ang, lusqa tu papal-u-un in saak.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: Duuq heza su-u lopa ko ka palkada-an tu kuzkuza-un?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: Ihaan saak pasnanava-an ta kuzakuza.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: Duuq kathaan amin uvavaz-az su-u ki asang ti?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: ni-i, ma-aq uvavaz-az naak a kathaan asangdengaz ta.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: Duuq untini-an asu ilumaq ti?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: Heza naak sinepuk tu tas-a asu siin tas-a qazam, maszang saam i tastulumaq.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: Heza naak amin sinepuk naung tu tas-a, ni-i tu maqanimulmul is heza sinapuk a uskunan.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: Asa saak masingav su-u ki pali-uni qanup tu is-atusasamu.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: Ni-i saak maqansiap a, ma-aq is heza naak qansepun a na taqu-ak asu.
ch: W:我知道的不多，但我能回答的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: ma-aq sia isqaqanup tu qemangsut a heza maqma-aq tu samu?
ch: M:你們打獵的器具有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: Ma-aq naam iskukusia qanup tu qemangsut a ni-i ka binano-az un maqtu matiduu.
ch: W:我們打獵的器具不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: Duuq maqtu ka binano-az un taskun qanup?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: Ni-i, ni-i ka binano-az un maqtu qanup.
ch: W:不行，女人也不可以打獵。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.
